title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Repository Trace: Gita Bundle Backup Verification
summary: A busy monorepo accumulates packs, stale worktrees, slow reachability walks, background jobs, and backup gaps while developers and CI operate concurrently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita bundle backup verification increment in measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Bundle Backup Verification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G64 is a theory in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Bundle Backup Verification contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G64 is a worked example in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. A defensible solution applies Gita Bundle Backup Verification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successfully created bundle necessarily contains every ref, reflog, hook, config, LFS object, and worktree needed for recovery. steps.title: Build Gita Bundle Backup Verification evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-r6s7zb" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Bundle Backup Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-r6s7zb
# scenario-key: trace-r6s7zb probe-bz7s6r boundary-s7zbr6 owner-r6s7zbx fault-r6s7zbz
# before-ref: main=a11cer6 candidate=b00b1zb
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11cer6 candidate=c0ffeeb
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
